summary: Executed backend auth-guard API tests (pytest) and frontend E2E flows for landing, login, dashboard, revenue, logs, reports, settings, onboarding on public URL. Core product flows are functional.

ui_bugs.component: Landing CTA + active sidebar nav item
ui_bugs.issue: Text appears invisible/very low contrast on light background (e.g., Create account button and active nav item render as blank in screenshots).

issues: Critical contrast/readability issue on light-background CTA/active nav states.

action_items: Fix CTA and active-navigation text contrast/readability for light-background variants.
Re-verify Tailwind class generation/theme tokens for text-zinc-950 usage in production build.
critical_code_review_comments: `/app/frontend/src/lib/prisma.ts` uses a fallback `process.env.DATABASE_URL ?? 'file:./dev.db'`; strict env policy requires failing fast when env var is missing (no default DB URL).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Demo account login works. Protected /rpc routes return 401 without session. Revenue/log/report/settings/onboarding flows exercised. Category restriction is enforced when fullLoggingEnabled=false (verified via authenticated API call returning 400 for MAINTENANCE).
rca of the issue: Reproduction: open landing page and authenticated dashboard sidebar; observe blank-looking text on light backgrounds. Likely root cause is theme/class token mismatch where intended dark text class on light surfaces is not applied at runtime. Mitigation: force explicit contrast-safe class (e.g., text-black) for light CTA/active states and validate in production build.
